context.description: A philosophical nonfiction passage explains how people become absorbed by symbol worlds and compares the path between symbolic realms to the Lorenz attractor. It argues that rejection of other reality models often reflects discomfort with contradictions in one's own model, then recommends Jonathan Pageau for studying Christian and universal symbolism.
normalization_notes: Classified as book_body_content due to continuous philosophical prose.
Normalized source-language summary and keywords into English.

rows.paragraph_text: Symbol worlds can absorb a person, and movement between symbolic realms is compared to an orbit-like path rather than a straight line. Resistance to other reality models is framed as avoidance of contradictions in one's own model, and Jonathan Pageau is recommended as a guide to Christian and universal symbolism.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Book page on symbolism, reality models, and universal religious symbolism. It uses the Lorenz attractor as a metaphor and recommends Jonathan Pageau.
